question.text: Regarding set-off and carry forward of capital losses from mutual fund investments, which of the following statements is CORRECT?

answer.text: Long-term capital loss can be set off against both long-term and short-term capital gains
Capital losses can be carried forward for a maximum of 4 assessment years
Long-term capital loss can be set off ONLY against long-term capital gains and carried forward for 8 assessment years
Short-term capital loss can be set off only against short-term capital gains

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** The set-off hierarchy is: (1) STCL can be set off against STCG and LTCG; (2) LTCL can be set off ONLY against LTCG. Unabsorbed capital losses (both STCL and LTCL) can be carried forward for 8 assessment years. The return must be filed on time to claim carry forward.
**The Trap:** Option A reverses the LTCL rule - LTCL cannot touch STCG. Option B uses 4 years instead of 8. Option D incorrectly restricts STCL to only STCG - STCL has wider set-off ability.
**Cross-Exam Context:** The asymmetry between STCL (can offset both) and LTCL (can offset only LTCG) is a fundamental concept. Also note: losses must be declared in the ITR filed before the due date to be eligible for carry forward.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Help clients understand that booking STCL is more flexible (can offset any capital gain), while LTCL is restricted. Year-end tax planning should consider this asymmetry.
